retrieved_docs.title: Aspergillosis -- Evaluation -- Biomarkers and Other Laboratory Investigations
retrieved_docs.content: Galactomannan can also be measured from bronchoalveolar lavage samples and has a sensitivity of 93.2% when an optical density of 0.5 is used. [53] Causes of false-positive galactomannan include blood transfusion, use of certain crystalloids in collecting bronchoalveolar lavage samples, and intravenous amoxicillin formulations, and it has been reported after eating ice pops. [49] [54] [51] There is also cross-reactivity with Fusarium, Histoplasma, Blastomyces, and Talaromyces species. [49] [51] 1,3-beta-D-glucan tests are very sensitive but are nonspecific as this is a component of the fungal cell wall and is not specific to Aspergillus species. [49] There are also many causes of false-positive tests, including membrane filters used for blood processing such as dialysis, drugs such as beta-lactams and beta-asparaginase, and contamination of blood collection tubes with glucan. [49] It should be noted that blood cultures are very rarely positive for Aspergillus species, even with a severe disease burden of disseminated disease. [55] PCR is not widely available, but further work is being done in this area. [55] [49]

retrieved_docs.title: Candidemia -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Because of the low sensitivity of blood cultures for Candida spp, surrogate markers can also be used to improve diagnostic accuracy. Considering all patient variables, these markers can be considered as supporting evidence when culture results are not yet available in patients at high risk for candidemia. Such markers are Candida mannan antigen, anti-mannan antibodies, and β-d-glucan (BDG). [32] [33] They have the advantage of providing faster results but can have high false-positive rates. The BDG can be used as a negative predictive value. [32] [34] When evaluating a patient suspected of candidemia, 2 sets of blood cultures should be obtained. A non-culture test (eg, BDG), should also be performed to enhance the diagnostic accuracy. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Fungal Endocarditis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The processing of blood cultures can be tedious and time-consuming. Quicker non-culture tests have been developed for the diagnosis of fungemia. Mannan antigen is a cell wall constituent of Candida spp. For candidemia, mannan antigen and antibody have a sensitivity and specificity of 83% and 86%, respectively. [13] Another cell wall polysaccharide of a fungal wall, 1,3 b-D-glucan, has a sensitivity and specificity of 69.9% and 87.1%, respectively. [11] Similarly, the detection of galactomannan, along with 1,3 b-D-glucan, can help to diagnose fungal endocarditis caused by Aspergillus species. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Febrile Neutropenia -- Evaluation -- Table 1. Bacteria cultured in febrile neutropenia
retrieved_docs.content: Additional testing may be required in some instances: For example, Galactomannan may be performed on patient serum, or bronchoalveolar lavage samples may serve as a diagnostic biomarker of fungal infections. 1,3-beta-D-glucan (BDG) is a fungal-specific cell wall component and may serve as a diagnostic biomarker of fungal infections. Procalcitonin, though still under investigation, may have moderate diagnostic accuracy for the detection of bacteremia in patients with sepsis. [10] Patients with unknown chest infiltrate on imaging may benefit from the culture of BAL fluid. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Fungal Endophthalmitis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Measuring levels of intraocular fluid beta-D-glucan was described by Chen et al. in 2020 as having a close association with fungal endophthalmitis and being more sensitive than culture methods. It is limited by an inability to confirm specific fungal species. [49]

retrieved_docs.title: Aspergillus Fumigatus (Archived) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: In cases of severe invasive aspergillosis an emerging tool has been used especially in intensive care unit patients, beta-(1,3)-d-glucan which is a cell wall component of Aspergillus and useful in cases where the onset of treatment is essential to improve prognosis. This component is also present in other organisms such as Candida , Fusarium , and Pneumocystis . Serum galactomannan is another Aspergillus cell wall component released into the bloodstream during intravascular hyphal growth. However, as it is captured by a neutrophil concentration that may be falsely low which makes its sensitivity lower in non-neutropenic patients. The key test for chronic pulmonary aspergillosis is a positive Aspergillus immunoglobin (IgG) serum test. Only a minority of patients have positive cultures (less than 10%). Elevated galactomannan assay from bronchoalveolar lavage fluid yields higher sensitivity in these cases. Other nonspecific markers are elevated CRP and ESR.

retrieved_docs.title: Fungal Infections of the Oral Mucosa -- Evaluation -- Candidiasis
retrieved_docs.content: Histopathology of the lesional tissue reveals the increased thickness of the parakeratin layer with embedded candidal hyphae, microabscess in the superficial spinous cell layer, and elongated rete ridges. The underlying connective tissue contains chronic inflammatory infiltrate. [46] Antifungal susceptibility testing is essential in patients who relapse or do not respond to conventional antifungals. [5] CHROMagar culture media can be used to isolate and identify Candida species . Production of germ tubes in the germ tube test indicates the presence of C albicans or C dubliniensis. Immunological methods to detect mannan antigen and anti-mannan antibodies, antimycelial antibodies, or β-D-glucan can be employed in immunocompromised patients when obtaining deep samples is challenging. Matrix-assisted laser desorption ionization-time of flight (MALDI-TOF) mass spectrometry reduces the culture time and is helpful in the identification of candidal species. [47]

retrieved_docs.title: Pneumocystis jirovecii Prophylaxis (Archived) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The diagnosis of PCP infection relies on clinical presentation and suspicion, presence of risk factors in combination with laboratory testing, imaging, sputum studies, which include microscopic detention of cysts and trophic form in respiratory secretion, evaluation of bronchoalveolar lavage fluid, or lung biopsy. Laboratory evaluation can be nonspecific, like elevated serum lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) and serum 1-3 beta D glucan. Beta D glucan can be found in the cell wall of most fungi, including Pneumocystis jirovecii . In the appropriate clinical context, serum LDH and 1-3 beta D glucan elevation should raise suspicion of PCP.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Sepsis -- Specimen Requirements and Procedure -- Fungal Specimens
retrieved_docs.content: Testing for fungal infection typically includes serological testing for the fungal cell wall components such as galactomannan, galactomannan antigen antibodies, and (1-3)-β-d-glucan. Other testing methods for fungal infection include reverse-transcriptase polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR) and culture of surveillance sites for Candida spp to differentiate between colonization and invasive Candida infection. Candida spp are cultured on Sabouraud dextrose agar with 0.05% chloramphenicol and chromogenic medium plates to identify polymicrobial cultures. [25] Cultures should be examined daily for up to 6 days before being considered negative. The diagnosis of invasive Aspergillus spp requires a positive culture of an otherwise sterile site or positive histopathology, as Aspergillus spp in respiratory tract secretions typically represent colonization and not necessarily an infectious process. Diagnostic methods for Mucorales are limited; the diagnosis is typically established with histopathology using hematoxylin-eosin, Grocott-methenamine-silver, and periodic acid-Schiff stains. [26] [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Aspergillosis -- Evaluation -- Biomarkers and Other Laboratory Investigations
retrieved_docs.content: Serum biomarkers such as galactomannan are helpful in the right population. It has the best-studied utility in patients with hematologic malignancy and hematopoietic stem cell transplant recipients, with an approximate 70% sensitivity. [49] Sensitivity decreases in other patient populations and is noted to be as low as 20% in the solid organ transplant population. [49] For this reason, serum galactomannan is recommended as a biomarker in patients with hematologic malignancy and hematopoietic stem cell transplant recipients not receiving mold prophylaxis. [49] [51] When an optical density of 0.5 is used, galactomannan is noted to have a sensitivity of 97.4% in detecting invasive aspergillosis or probable invasive aspergillosis. [52]

retrieved_docs.title: Fungal Keratitis -- Evaluation -- Corneal Biopsy and Polymerase Chain Reaction
retrieved_docs.content: Beta-D-glucan, a component of the cell wall found in many fungal species, can be identified within the bloodstream of patients with invasive fungal infections. [44] Previous research has demonstrated that beta-d-glucan testing outpaces fungal cultures regarding speed and sensitivity when diagnosing systemic fungal infections, particularly in cases where the organisms don't thrive in blood cultures, such as Aspergillosis . [45] The presence of beta-D-glucan has the potential to be detected in tear samples from individuals affected by fungal keratitis, expediting early and prompt diagnosis. [46] Furthermore, it can also be seen in intraocular fluids of cases complicated by fungal endophthalmitis. [47]

retrieved_docs.title: Lactose Intolerance -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials -- Fungal Beta-Galactosidases
retrieved_docs.content: Two fungal beta-galactosidases derived from Aspergillus carbonarius ATCC6276 (beta-gal 1 and beta-gal 2) have shown potential as enzyme supplements for lactose intolerance. Administered alone or in combination, these purified enzymes demonstrate greater stability under simulated gastric conditions compared to current commercial lactase preparations. [51]

retrieved_docs.title: Obesity Supplements -- Function -- Beta-Glucans
retrieved_docs.content: Beta-glucans are soluble fibers found in bacteria, yeast, fungi, and grains such as oat bran and barley. They are glucose polysaccharides fermented by the gut microbiota. [3] Weight loss effects may be due to increased satiety and decreased food intake. A meta-analysis published in 2019 showed that most trials reported non-significant or no weight loss from beta-glucans administered at a dose of 3 to 10 grams per day for 4 to 12 weeks. [4] Data does not currently support their use for the treatment of obesity. Adverse effects include increased flatulence. [5] Camellia Sinensis

retrieved_docs.title: Caspofungin -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: Caspofungin, along with other echinocandins, works by noncompetitive inhibition of the enzyme β-(1,3)-D-glucan synthase, a critical component in the synthesis of the fungal cell wall; this enzyme is not present in mammalian cells. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Gyromitra Mushroom Toxicity -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The diagnosis of Gyromitra syndrome is clinical, relying on associating the patient’s liver, renal, and CNS findings with a history of Gyromitra mushroom ingestion. Specific tests for gyromitrin detection are currently unavailable in the clinical setting.

retrieved_docs.title: Antifungal Agents -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: Echinocandins (eg, caspofungin) inhibit the fungal β-(1,3)-D-glucan synthase, which is the enzyme responsible for synthesizing β-(1,3)-D-glucan, a key component of fungal cell walls. Losing this cell wall component leads to osmotic instability and cell death. [7] Griseofulvin is a mitotic inhibitor that binds to polymerized fungal microtubules, thereby inhibiting depolymerization and leading to the failure of fungal cell replication. [40]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1341
retrieved_docs.content: Beta‑D‑glucopyranose is classified as a saccharide, belonging to the broad group of carbohydrate molecules known as saccharidy. As a specific sugar monomer, beta‑D‑glucopyranose exemplifies the structural and functional characteristics of saccharides, which serve as essential energy sources, metabolic intermediates, and structural components in biological systems. Recognizing beta‑D‑glucopyranose as a member of the saccharidy class aids in understanding its role in nutrition, biochemical pathways, and laboratory analyses of carbohydrate content.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2494
retrieved_docs.content: Triazole derivatives, systemic antimycotics constitute a class of antifungal agents that are administered systemically to treat invasive fungal infections. Both itraconazole and voriconazole are members of this triazole derivative class, sharing the characteristic triazole ring structure that confers broad-spectrum activity against a variety of pathogenic fungi. Their classification as systemic antimycotics underscores their use in managing serious fungal diseases where topical therapy is insufficient, highlighting the importance of these agents in comprehensive antifungal treatment strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1621
retrieved_docs.content: Glucose 100 mg/mL injection is formulated with dextrose as its pharmacologically active component; anhydrous dextrose constitutes the active moiety of the preparation, while dextrose monohydrate serves as the active ingredient. Both forms of dextrose provide the therapeutic glucose delivered by the injection, ensuring consistent dosing and efficacy in clinical use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1656
retrieved_docs.content: Terconazole may be used to prevent عدوى فطرية (fungal infection).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 1 -- Community 85
retrieved_docs.content: the pharmacologically active component is anhydrous dextrose (the active moiety), while dextrose monohydrate is listed as the active ingredient; together these anhydrous and monohydrate forms constitute the essential active substance of the formulation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1985
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc supplements are classified as part of zinc gluconate, indicating that zinc gluconate represents a specific formulation within the broader category of zinc supplements.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 2 -- Community 894
retrieved_docs.content: Triazole derivatives that are used as systemic antimycotics constitute a pharmacologic class that includes itraconazole, posaconazole, and voriconazole. These agents share the triazole chemical scaffold and are employed for the treatment of systemic fungal infections. In addition to its antifungal activity, posaconazole is also identified as a trypanocidal compound, indicating that it possesses activity against trypanosome parasites. This dual activity of posaconazole exemplifies the broader antimicrobial potential that can be found within the triazole derivative class.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 569
retrieved_docs.content: Anhydrous dextrose serves as the active moiety of the medication glucose 100 MG/ML Injection, meaning it is the pharmacologically effective component of this injectable glucose solution used to provide rapid carbohydrate supplementation in clinical settings.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1464
retrieved_docs.content: Triazole derivatives, systemic antimycotics are classified as systemic antimycotics, representing a pharmacologic class of antifungal agents used for systemic infections. Within this class, itraconazole and voriconazole are specific triazole derivative compounds that belong to the group of systemic antimycotics, exemplifying the members of this therapeutic category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 1 -- Community 117
retrieved_docs.content: antifungal and is marketed as a 250 mg oral tablet, illustrating its role in systemic therapy; the same active ingredient appears as an inactive component within methylcelulosa, the parent class of the demulcent formulation, demonstrating a formulation link between demulcent bases and specific antifungal agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1292
retrieved_docs.content: Acarbose is chemically related to trisaccharides, as it has trisaccharides as its parent structure. This classification indicates that acarbose is derived from or belongs to the trisaccharide family, which underlies its function as an alpha‑glucosidase inhibitor used to slow carbohydrate digestion in the management of diabetes. Understanding that acarbose originates from trisaccharides helps explain its mechanism of action and informs its appropriate use in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1484
retrieved_docs.content: Imidazole derivatives, antimycotic for systemic use, are classified as a type of antimycotics for systemic use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 1642
retrieved_docs.content: ANTIFUNGALS FOR DERMATOLOGICAL USE constitute a broad class of antifungal agents employed in the treatment of skin disorders, and within this class, antifungals for topical use represent the specific subset of medications applied directly to the skin surface to manage superficial fungal infections. Recognizing that topical antifungals are a component of the larger dermatological antifungal category helps clinicians select appropriate therapy based on the site and severity of infection.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 1448
retrieved_docs.content: Migalastat is identified as a saccharide, indicating that this therapeutic agent belongs to the class of carbohydrate-derived compounds.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2166
retrieved_docs.content: Butenafine, an antifungal medication, is contraindicated in patients who have فرط تحسس (hypersensitivity), meaning it should not be prescribed to individuals with a known allergic or hypersensitivity reaction to avoid adverse immune responses.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 1 -- Community 29
retrieved_docs.content: resin 4000 mg oral suspension. Migalastat and beta‑D‑glucopyranose are also classified as carbohydrates. Aztreonam 1000 mg injection exemplifies standard dosing conventions by using grams as the unit of presentation strength. These interrelated active ingredients, therapeutic indications and contraindications illustrate the necessity of considering drug classes, disease‑specific contraindications and overlapping treatment options to optimize patient care.
